questions.question: What are Git tags primarily used for?
options: Creating new branches for feature development
Marking specific commits as release points
Tracking which files have been modified
questions.answer: Marking specific commits as release points

questions.question: Which command creates an annotated tag?
What is the key difference between an annotated tag and a lightweight tag?
options: Lightweight tags can be pushed to remotes but annotated tags cannot
Annotated tags contain metadata (tagger, date, message) while lightweight tags are just a name pointing to a commit
Lightweight tags support semantic versioning but annotated tags do not
Annotated tags can only be created on the HEAD commit
questions.answer: Annotated tags contain metadata (tagger, date, message) while lightweight tags are just a name pointing to a commit

questions.question: Which type of tag is recommended for marking releases?
In Semantic Versioning (SemVer), what does the version format MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH represent?
According to SemVer, when should the PATCH version be incremented?
options: When bug fixes are made with no new features
questions.answer: When bug fixes are made with no new features

questions.question: According to SemVer, when should the MAJOR version be incremented?
options: When minor bug fixes are applied
When new backwards-compatible features are added
When the project reaches a milestone

questions.question: Which command creates a lightweight tag named 'beta-test'?
What command lists all existing tags in a repository?
Which command filters tags to show only those matching a pattern like 'v1.*'?
What does 'git show v1.0.0' display for an annotated tag?
options: The tag metadata AND the commit it points to
A list of all files changed since the tag was created
The branch the tag was created on
questions.answer: The tag metadata AND the commit it points to

questions.question: What does 'git show' display when used on a lightweight tag?
options: The tagger name, date, and tag message
An error because lightweight tags cannot be shown
Just the commit information with no tag metadata
The full history of all commits since the tag
questions.answer: Just the commit information with no tag metadata

questions.question: What happens when you check out a tag with 'git checkout v1.0.0'?
options: A new branch named v1.0.0 is created automatically
The tag is deleted and replaced with a branch
Git refuses because tags cannot be checked out

questions.question: After checking out a tag and entering detached HEAD state, how do you return to your branch?
Are tags pushed to a remote automatically when you run 'git push'?
options: Yes, all tags are always pushed with every push
No, tags must be pushed explicitly
Only annotated tags are pushed automatically
Only lightweight tags are pushed automatically
questions.answer: No, tags must be pushed explicitly

questions.question: Which command pushes a single tag named v1.0.0 to the remote?
Which command pushes all local tags to the remote at once?
Which command deletes a tag locally?
Which command deletes a tag from a remote repository?
How do you tag a past commit rather than the current HEAD?
options: You cannot tag past commits; tags only apply to HEAD

questions.question: What is a GitHub Release?
options: A branch that is marked as production-ready
A way to package a tagged version with release notes, changelogs, and downloadable archives
A special commit that cannot be reverted
A pull request that has been merged into main
questions.answer: A way to package a tagged version with release notes, changelogs, and downloadable archives

questions.question: What is the first step to create a GitHub Release?
options: Run 'git release create' from the command line
Push a branch named 'release' to GitHub
Go to your repository on GitHub and click 'Releases'
questions.answer: Go to your repository on GitHub and click 'Releases'

questions.question: Which command shows the commit log with tag names displayed next to their commits?
If a project goes from v1.0.0 to v1.1.0, what kind of change occurred according to SemVer?
options: A new backwards-compatible feature was added
questions.answer: A new backwards-compatible feature was added

questions.question: When creating a GitHub Release, what must you select to associate it with a specific point in your project history?
